Title: Cytosolic lipid droplets as engineered organelles for production and accumulation of terpenoid biomaterials in leaves

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: lipid droplet structural sequestration platform for trapping hydrophobic terpenoids
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly enhanced AtWRI11-397-induced TAG/lipid droplet accumulation and enabled lipid droplet targeting of fusion enzymes; promoted diterpenoid sequestration
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression with AtWRI11-397; TAG measurement; confocal microscopy; LD fusion localization; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In some combinations clustering of small lipid droplets occurred; high LD engineering could reduce sesquiterpenoid or TAG yields depending on pathway stack
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful structural module to create synthetic lipid droplets and anchor enzymes for metabolite sequestration/compartmentalization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NoLDSP synergized with AtWRI11-397 to increase TAG levels from ~3-fold to ~12-fold over controls. 2. It served as a lipid-droplet anchor for AgABS, PsCYP720B4, and CaCPR fusion proteins. 3. Lipid droplet-associated NoLDSP helped sequester diterpenoids and created a synthetic organelle platform for terpenoid engineering.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoted clustering of small lipid droplets; functions partially analogous to plant oleosins.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf tissue; sequestered in cytosolic lipid droplets when co-engineered
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol or plastid when transit peptide-fused
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in leaves; 4 days after infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables patchoulol production in N. benthamiana; plastid targeting plus precursor engineering gave the highest retained patchoulol levels
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in N. benthamiana; subcellular retargeting; co-expression with precursor-supply genes; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Cytosolic patchoulol production combined with lipid droplet engineering reduced triacylglycerol accumulation; cytosolic co-engineering impaired patchoulol accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful terminal synthase for sesquiterpenoid engineering; best deployed with plastid targeting and co-expression of DXS and FDPS, and potentially lipid droplet sequestration
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcPAS was the core sesquiterpene synthase used to produce patchoulol in leaves. 2. Cytosolic PcPAS alone produced low patchoulol, while ElHMGR159-582 plus AtFDPS increased yield about 5-fold. 3. Plastid-targeted PcPAS with PbDXS and plastid AtFDPS increased patchoulol about 60-fold, and with AtWRI11-397 plus NoLDSP reached the highest retained levels (~45 µg g^-1 fresh weight).
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Patchoulol is volatile; engineering strategy aimed to retain/sequester it in biomass rather than quantify emission losses.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf tissue; lipid droplets under co-engineering context
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: FDP supply module for sesquiterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol or plastid when transit peptide-fused
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses isoprenoid C5 units to form farnesyl diphosphate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhanced patchoulol accumulation when co-expressed with PcPAS and upstream precursor-boosting genes; plastid-targeted form contributed to highest patchoulol retention
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression with PcPAS in different compartments; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In cytosolic high-yield patchoulol stacks, TAG accumulation was reduced when combined with lipid droplet engineering
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply gene for sesquiterpenoid engineering, especially with plastid-targeted PcPAS and DXS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtFDPS served as the FDP-supplying enzyme for engineered patchoulol production. 2. Cytosolic AtFDPS together with ElHMGR159-582 increased patchoulol from cytosolic PcPAS. 3. Plastid-targeted AtFDPS together with PbDXS strongly enhanced plastid PcPAS output and supported the highest patchoulol accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chosen partly for relatively high thermal stability.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf tissue; lipid droplets for diterpenoid sequestration context
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: cytosolic precursor supply for sesqui-/diterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces HMG-CoA to mevalonate, increasing flux through the mevalonate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as rate-limiting/key control step in the MEV pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Boosted cytosolic terpenoid production when combined with downstream synthases; enabled strong increases in patchoulol and cytosolic diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; truncated feedback-insensitive form; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In cytosolic high-yield terpenoid plus lipid-droplet stacks, TAG levels decreased
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong upstream engineering target for cytosolic terpenoid production, particularly in stacked pathways
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Truncated ElHMGR159-582 enhanced precursor flux through the cytosolic mevalonate pathway. 2. Co-expression with AtFDPS and PcPAS increased patchoulol about 5-fold over PcPAS alone. 3. Co-expression with cytosol:MtGGDPS and cytosol:AgABS85-868 increased diterpenoid yield by >400-fold over cytosol:AgABS85-868 alone.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The truncated form is described as feedback-insensitive/de-regulated and robust.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf tissue; lipid droplets for diterpenoid sequestration context
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plastidial precursor supply for sesqui-/diterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses pyruvate and D-glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate to form 1-deoxy-D-xylulose 5-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as rate-limiting in the MEP pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly enhanced plastid-targeted patchoulol and diterpenoid production when combined with appropriate downstream prenyltransferases and synthases
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; compartment engineering; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: When plastid-targeted high-yield diterpenoid pathway was combined with high lipid droplet production, diterpenoid yield decreased and TAG trended lower, consistent with competition for plastid carbon
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key plastid precursor-supply gene for terpenoid engineering; effective in multigene stacks with prenyltransferases and terminal synthases
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbDXS was used as the plastid precursor-boosting enzyme. 2. Together with plastid AtFDPS, it increased plastid PcPAS-derived patchoulol about 60-fold. 3. Together with plastid GGDPSs and AgABS, it enabled the highest plastidial diterpenoid yields.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The PbDXS cDNA used differed from a previously published sequence and was newly deposited.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: abietadiene and isomers; diterpenoid acids when coupled to CYP720B4
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: diterpenoid biomaterials and precursors of resin acids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf tissue; strongly enriched in lipid droplet fractions under lipid droplet engineering
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bifunctional diterpene synthase, class II/I terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid (native), Cytosol (AgABS85-868), or lipid droplet surface (LD:AgABS85-868)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranylgeranyl diphosphate via (+)-copalyl diphosphate to abietadiene and related isomers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produced diterpenoid scaffolds in plastid, cytosol, and lipid droplets; yields increased with precursor engineering and were enhanced by lipid droplet sequestration in selected contexts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression and retargeting to plastid/cytosol/lipid droplets; metabolite profiling by GC-MS and UHPLC/MS/MS; localization of LD fusion
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PbDXS; MtGGDPS; SaGGDPS; TsGGDPS; EpGGDPS1; EpGGDPS2; MeGGDPS; PsCYP720B4; CaCPR70-708; AtWRI11-397; NoLDSP
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In some plastid high-yield plus lipid droplet stacks, diterpenoid yield decreased and TAG trended lower
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Versatile diterpene scaffold synthase for engineering resin diterpenoids; can be deployed in plastid, cytosol, or anchored to lipid droplets depending pathway design
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Native plastid AgABS alone produced ~40 µg g^-1 fresh weight diterpenoids in leaves. 2. Co-expression with PbDXS and selected GGDPSs increased yield up to 6.5-fold. 3. Cytosolic AgABS85-868 plus ElHMGR159-582 and cytosol:MtGGDPS increased diterpenoids >400-fold over cytosolic AgABS alone. 4. LD-targeted AgABS85-868 remained catalytically active and supported diterpenoid/resin acid production at lipid droplets.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bifunctional enzyme with both class II and class I terpene synthase activities.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf tissue; lipid droplets under co-engineering context
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: GGDP supply module for diterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes geranylgeranyl diphosphate from isoprenoid C5 precursors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: One of the most effective GGDPSs for boosting diterpenoid production in plastid and cytosol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transient co-expression with AgABS and PbDXS or ElHMGR159-582; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No additive benefit in some fully stacked lipid droplet high-yield cytosolic combinations; plastid high-yield stack plus high LD could reduce diterpenoid yield
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Robust alternative GGDPS for engineering diterpenoid flux and potentially bypassing native regulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtGGDPS functioned as an effective GGDP-supplying enzyme for diterpenoid biosynthesis. 2. In plastids, co-expression with PbDXS and AgABS gave among the highest diterpenoid yields. 3. In the cytosol, co-expression with ElHMGR159-582 and AgABS85-868 raised diterpenoid accumulation by >400-fold relative to AgABS85-868 alone.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion highlights type I MtGGDPS as a robust alternative to plant type II GGDPSs to circumvent potential feedback.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf tissue; free and glycosylated forms detected
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum (native), Cytosol (truncated), or lipid droplet surface (NoLDSP fusion)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes abietadiene and related diterpene isomers to corresponding diterpene resin acids
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: diterpenoid resin acids including abietic acid-type products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converted AgABS-derived diterpenes to diterpenoid acids; ER-localized native form generally outperformed cytosolic or LD-targeted truncated versions in this system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression with AgABS variants and CaCPR; product detection by GC-MS/UHPLC-MS/MS; localization of LD fusion proteins
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: LD-targeted configuration produced lower diterpenoid acid levels than native ER-targeted CYP in several combinations
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful oxidation enzyme for functionalized diterpenoid production, but subcellular context is critical; ER deployment may currently be preferable to LD retargeting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsCYP720B4 converted AgABS-derived diterpenes into diterpenoid resin acids in N. benthamiana. 2. Native ER-targeted PsCYP720B4 consistently gave high diterpenoid acid accumulation. 3. Truncated cytosolic and LD-targeted PsCYP720B4 remained functional but were generally less effective than the ER-targeted enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Products accumulated in both free and glycosylated forms due to endogenous detoxification/glycosylation in N. benthamiana.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: electron transfer to CYP720B4 on lipid droplets
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers electrons from NADPH to cytochrome P450 for oxidative diterpenoid functionalization
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for functionalization of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled LD-localized PsCYP720B4 activity for production of diterpenoid acids, though yields were generally below ER-targeted CYP system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression as LD fusion with LD:PsCYP720B4 and AgABS variants; confocal localization; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: LD-targeted oxidation module was functional but not optimal relative to native ER-based oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful redox partner when constructing synthetic CYP modules in non-native compartments such as lipid droplets
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LD:CaCPR70-708 co-localized with LD:PsCYP720B4 on lipid droplets. 2. Its inclusion supported CYP-mediated conversion of diterpenes to diterpenoid acids at lipid droplets. 3. The LD-targeted redox module was functional but generally yielded less product than native ER-targeted PsCYP720B4.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: triacylglycerol; indirectly patchoulol and diterpenoids via lipid droplet engineering
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: TAG storage oil; engineered sequestration platform aiding terpenoid retention
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/EREBP family transcription factor; truncated stabilized form
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: fatty acid biosynthesis and lipid droplet-associated TAG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: fatty acid biosynthesis network genes in plastids
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates fatty acid biosynthetic program, promoting TAG/lipid droplet accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased leaf TAG about 3-fold alone and, with NoLDSP, about 12-fold; lipid droplet formation altered terpenoid yields depending on pathway design and promoted diterpenoid sequestration
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in N. benthamiana; TAG quantification; combinatorial terpenoid pathway engineering
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key master regulator used to generate synthetic lipid droplets that trap produced terpenoids and modulate product retention
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competition with plastid terpenoid synthesis or altered carbon partitioning reduced TAG or terpenoid yields in some stacks; cytosolic high-yield terpenoid combinations significantly lowered TAG
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Master regulator for vegetative oil engineering and terpenoid trapping; likely best used with carefully tuned promoters in stable lines to reduce interference with growth.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtWRI11-397 initiated TAG accumulation in leaves and was central to synthetic lipid droplet formation. 2. Co-expression with NoLDSP raised TAG accumulation to ~12-fold over controls. 3. In combination with terpenoid pathways, it enhanced diterpenoid sequestration in lipid droplets and influenced product yields positively or negatively depending on compartment and precursor supply.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The truncated 1-397 version was selected because removal of an intrinsically disordered region increases protein stability according to prior work.
